subject: Social Science - History
chapter_name: Civilising the Native, Educating the Nation
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the structure of indigenous education systems before colonial rule
Examine British attitudes towards Indian knowledge systems
Explain the Anglicist–Orientalist debate in colonial education policy
Interpret the significance of Macaulay’s Minute (1835)
Understand the reforms introduced by Wood’s Despatch (1854)
Evaluate the role of Indian reformers in promoting modern education
Assess the impact of colonial education on Indian society and nationalism

lesson_plan.title: Indigenous Education Before British Rule
topics_covered: Role of pathshalas, gurukuls, and madrassas
teaching_strategy: Use of diagrams to illustrate learning systems
student_activities: Create a chart listing features of indigenous schools
Discuss how education was supported by local communities

lesson_plan.title: British Attitudes Toward Indian Education
topics_covered: Introduction of Western knowledge and science
Education as a tool for administration
student_activities: Identify reasons the British promoted Western education
Discuss advantages and disadvantages of colonial education

lesson_plan.title: Anglicists and Orientalists Debate
topics_covered: Impact of the debate on education policy
student_activities: Prepare arguments for Anglicists or Orientalists
Participate in a short classroom debate

lesson_plan.title: Macaulay’s Minute and Education Policy
topics_covered: Promotion of English as the medium of instruction
student_activities: Write a summary of Macaulay’s arguments

lesson_plan.title: Wood’s Despatch and Modern Education
student_activities: Mark the universities established in 1857 on a map
List the major reforms suggested in Wood’s Despatch

lesson_plan.title: Indian Reformers and Education
topics_covered: Raja Rammohan Roy’s views on education
Promotion of scientific and modern learning
student_activities: Prepare a short biography of Raja Rammohan Roy
Discuss how education influences social change

lesson_plan.title: Impact of Colonial Education and Revision
student_activities: List positive and negative impacts of colonial education

teacher_tip: Encourage students to critically compare indigenous education with colonial education. Using debates and map activities can help students better understand the historical changes in education policy.
